channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft’s quantum ambition: One million qubits in the palm of your hand
item.description: Microsoft announced a breakthrough in quantum computing today, unveiling a new kind of quantum processing unit, using a new type of material, to create what it says is a “radically different type of qubit.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: This portable Hi-Res Audio music player has a tube amp and Android, for a serious mix of the retro and modern
item.description: If you want to enjoy Hi-Res audio on the go, then this new music player from Shanling may be one to check out.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Más allá del ‘hype’: cuatro casos de uso de IA generativa que sí han funcionado
item.description: Si 2023 fue el año de la experimentación con la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa, 2024 fue cuando las empresas se centraron en casos de uso y comenzaron a poner en producción proyectos piloto.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Experts give up hope for 157 false killer whales stranded on a beach in Australia's Tasmania state
item.description: Marine experts have given up hope of rescuing more than 150 false killer whales that stranded on a remote beach on Australia’s island state of Tasmania.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What is Y Combinator now? Critics say the famed accelerator is having an identity crisis
item.description: In December, Y Combinator’s first-ever Fall batch got their own Demo Day. The Silicon Valley-based startup accelerator—which has produced big hits like Airbnb, Doordash, and Stripe—had doubled the number of startup classes that could enter its program.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Bannon: Musk 'wants to impose his freak experiments' on US
item.description: Former White House chief adviser turned conservative commentator Steve Bannon blasted billionaire Elon Musk over his efforts to reduce the size of the federal government and gain more power in the U.S. "Musk is a parasitic illegal immigrant," he said during an interview with the website UnHerd.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Don't let AI phantom hackers drain your bank account
item.description: Attackers use caller ID spoofing and AI voices to steal money. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides seven ways to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French AI Startup Mistral Shifts Focus to Regional Language LLMs
item.description: French AI startup Mistral is turning its focus toward providing large language models (LLMs) that understand regional languages and their parlance, driven by rising demand among enterprise customers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Neudesic launches in Australia in a bid to speed up AI adoption for IT teams
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) has gained significant traction among business leaders keen to explore ways it can drive operational efficiencies and cost savings. But while top leadership is sold on its potential, it’s a different tale for IT teams working the ground.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to keep AI hallucinations out of your code
item.description: It turns out androids do dream, and their dreams are often strange. In the early days of generative AI, we got human hands with eight fingers and recipes for making pizza sauce from glue. Now, developers working with AI-assisted coding tools are also finding AI hallucinations in their code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
